key_insight: Sliding-window attention can become competitive for math reasoning when it is converted from a pretrained self-attention model and then adapted with reinforcement learning.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to cut inference cost and memory from quadratic self-attention while retaining much of the reasoning accuracy needed for long-context LLM deployment.
method: Convert a pretrained self-attention model to sliding-window attention and fine-tune with supervised data.
Apply on-policy reinforcement learning under the sliding-window constraint to adapt generated trajectories.
Use architecture-aware adaptation to reduce the mismatch between training data and the restricted attention pattern.
evidence_signal: Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks show that RL narrows the accuracy gap between sliding-window and self-attention models, recovering much of the loss introduced by conversion and SFT.
watch_item: The approach may be sensitive to the quality and architecture-match of training data, since SFT examples designed for self-attention can contain long-range dependencies that sliding-window models struggle to represent.
paper_title: Architecture-Aware Reinforcement Learning Makes Sliding-Window Attention Competitive in Math Reasoning
